findings.message: Cache should be cleaned after package installation. Package manager cache should be removed to reduce Docker image size. Use 'apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*' for apt, 'yum clean all' for yum, 'dnf clean all' for dnf, or '--no-cache' flag for apk.

Consent flag should be set to avoid manual input. Package manager commands should include consent flags to enable non-interactive execution. Use '-n' or '--non-interactive' for gdebi, '-y' for apt/apt-get/yum/dnf, '--non-interactive' for zypper, or '--noconfirm' for pacman.

Credentials should not be hard-coded. Hard-coded credentials in Dockerfiles pose a security risk as they can be exposed in source code repositories and container images. Use Docker secrets, environment variables passed at runtime, or secure secret management systems instead.
findings.value: export TEMP_PASSWORD=mytemp123 && echo "Using password: $TEMP_PASSWORD" && unset TEMP_PASSWORD

Missing mandatory descriptive label(s): maintainer, version, description. Add LABEL instructions for better image documentation (e.g., LABEL maintainer="email@example.com" version="1.0" description="App description").

Variable references should be enclosed in double quotes to prevent globbing and word splitting. Unquoted variables can cause issues when they contain whitespaces or shell glob characters (*). Use "$VAR" or "${VAR}" instead of $VAR or ${VAR}.
findings.value: export TEMP_PASSWORD=mytemp123 && echo "Using password: $TEMP_PASSWORD" && unset TEMP_PASSWORD

Environment variable 'API_KEY' was set with ENV at line 11 but unset with RUN at line 19 on a different layer. The variable remains accessible from the previous layer. Consider using ARG instead of ENV for build-time secrets, or use RUN export/unset in the same command as ENV.
